intro: Gothofreda incana, also known as Black Horehound, is a perennial plant species within the Lamiaceae family, native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Gothofreda incana contains bioactive constituents like terpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Gothofreda incana include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to alleviate colds, fever, and other respiratory complaints.
